_comment: Medical Oncology package for Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma
procedure_name: CT for Low Grade NHL - Rituximab + Cyclophosphamide + Vincristine + Prednisolone
min_doctor_qualification: DM (Medical oncology/ Hematology) or equivalent (MD Medicine with experience or Fellowship in Hematology/Medical oncology)

preauth.label: Biopsy or bone marrow or peripheral blood flow cytometry report for low grade NHL
Evidence to support indication of therapy in low grade lymphoma (as in SLL/CLL document)
Stage of disease – CECT chest +abdomen +Pelvis or PET-CT
Clinical notes with performance status and disease sites including extranodal sites
Viral markers – HbsAg, HCV and HIV

claim.label: Reports of the tests (pathology, radiology, microbiology, hematology, biochemistry, etc.)
Discharge summary of day care department
Documentation of response after 3 to 4 cycles either with PET-CT or CECT (same as done at diagnosis) and clinical notes

ppd_preauth.q: Is there any contraindication as listed?

cpd_claim.q: Is there no response assessment or progression after 3-4 cycles?

common_queries: Diagnosis of Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma (Adults) is uncertain.
No response assessment or progression after 3-4 cycles of treatment.
Missing biopsy or bone marrow or peripheral blood flow cytometry report for low grade NHL.
Lack of evidence to support the indication of therapy in low grade lymphoma.
Incomplete or missing reports for staging (CECT/PET-CT) or routine tests (CBC, LFT, RFT, Viral markers) at pre-authorization.
Absence of drug barcodes or detailed discharge summary at claim submission.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Essential Medical Personnel: Qualified Medical Oncologist/ Hematologist. Essential Medical Infrastructure: Chemotherapy unit/ Ward/ Day care/ Injection room/ LINEAC
clinical_key_pointers: For low grade lymphomas, package can be repeated as a 2nd line too, depending on duration and patient's general condition at relapse (if there is response to the same regimen earlier and after a gap of more than 1 year. Not permitted more than twice for any patient).
The indications for therapy in CLL, SLL, FL, Marginal zone lymphoma and other low-grade lymphomas as per the NCG guidelines.
